text:
Channel Nine presenter Karl Stefanovic has launched into a fiery rant about French people while covering the Olympic Games in Paris.

Channel 9 presenter Karl Stefanovic has delivered a blunt review of French people, describing them as "weird", "rude" and sexually uncouth in a withering assessment of the European nation.

Stefanovic, 49, delivered the critical verdict to the Kyle and Jackie O show on Monday following a stint in Paris covering the Olympic Games for the embattled Australian broadcaster.

“The French are weirdos. The French are complete weirdos. We all know that,” he said.

“They’re kind of a weird nation, sexually.”

Henderson asked Stefanovic if he exuded his French speaking skills in the nation’s capital, which he promptly denied.

“Do you know what? I’ve stopped trying. I don’t really like them that much,” he said.

The outspoken presenter doubled down on his disdain for the country with his only positive appraisal coming from the cuisine.

“They’re so rude. Oh, they’re really up themselves… they’re pompous. They do a good burger though. I do like the burgers and stuff over here,” he said,

Stefanovic, who hosted the Games’ opening ceremony, said he enjoyed the “blue Smurf with his aggots hanging out”, adding “I mean, it just doesn’t get better”.

The Sydney-born media personality’s wife of six years Jasmine, 40, and daughter Harper accompanied him to the Paris Games in luxury Airbnb sponsored accommodation.

Jasmine on Instagram uploaded a photo with their daughter smiling on a hotel balcony during their lavish funded stay.

“A beautiful welcome to Paris by @airbnb thank you for hosting us during the @olympics. We’ve made such amazing family memories already. It truly feels like a home away from home,” the mother-of-one wrote.

She later copped flack after she uploaded a photo of the couple arm in arm with the Paris Eiffel tower in the background ahead of a dinner sponsored by Channel 9.

"La Girafe with you," the shoe designer captioned the picture.

"Thank you @channel9 for a beautiful dinner."

Girafe Restaurant is described as an upscale eatery, situated near the Palais de Chaillot with views of the Eiffel Tower, and specialises in gourmet seafood dishes.

Menu items such as caviar cost up to AUD$1,000 and a seafood platter for two starring lobster is priced at almost $500, according to the menu on its website.

Jasmine was called out for the post which attracted criticism and anger from social media users arguing the dinner was poor timing given the negative spotlight on the network in recent weeks.

"I'm sure all the people who have just been sacked from Channel 9 will be happy to see you eating at the most expensive restaurant in Paris paid for by Channel 9," one wrote.

"Like read the room. Go have dinner and enjoy yourself. But at least have enough compassion to not post about it,” said another.

It comes as Nine last month announced hundreds of job cuts as part of the network's cost-cutting plan - which will eliminate five per cent of the company’s total work force, mostly from its metro newspapers and the Nine TV network.

The company attributed the cuts to the termination of a commercial deal with Meta, the owner of Facebook and Instagram, as well as a sluggish advertising market.

The axed Meta deal -which compensated Australian news publishers for content used on social media- was a lifeline for struggling networks and newspapers due to the decline in free-to-air linear broadcast audiences and shrinking newspaper circulations.

title: Karl Stefanovic says the French are 'rude', pompous' and 'weird sexually' in shocking tirade while covering Olympic Games in Paris | Sky News Australia
description: Channel 9 presenter Karl Stefanovic has delivered a blunt review of French people, describing them as "weird", "rude" and sexually uncouth in a withering assessment of the European nation.
